Now we need to prove `range (val_image U A) = 𝒫 A`.

The left side is `range (val_image U A)`, which is the set of all outputs of `val_image U A` as its argument `X` varies over `Set A`.

The right side is `𝒫 A`, which is the power set of `A`, i.e., the set of all subsets of `A`.

So we need to prove `range (val_image U A) = 𝒫 A`.

Let's expand the definition. `range (val_image U A)` is the set of all sets `Y` such that `Y = val_image U A X` for some `X : Set A`.

`val_image U A X = {y : U | ∃ x ∈ X, x.val = y}`.

So we need to prove that the set of all such sets equals `𝒫 A`.

So we need to prove `∀ Y, Y ∈ range (val_image U A) ↔ Y ∈ 𝒫 A`.

Which is `∀ Y, (∃ X : Set A, val_image U A X = Y) ↔ Y ⊆ A`.

This is the theorem we need to prove.

The right side is `𝒫 A`, which is the power set of `A`, i.e., the set of all subsets of `A`.
